title: House of Bolkiah

infobox.value: [Qatadah](/wiki/Banu_Qatadah "Banu Qatadah") of [al-Hasani](/wiki/Hasanids "Hasanids") of [al-Alawi](/wiki/Alids "Alids") of [al-Hashimi](/wiki/Banu_Hashim "Banu Hashim") of [al-Qurashi](/wiki/Quraysh "Quraysh") of [al-Adnani](/wiki/Adnanites "Adnanites") of [Ishmael in Islam](/wiki/Ishmael_in_Islam "Ishmael in Islam") of [Abraham in Islam](/wiki/Abraham_in_Islam "Abraham in Islam")  * (since 1425 due to [cognatic inheritance](/wiki/Sharif_Ali#Accession "Sharif Ali"))
[Sultan Haji Hassanal Bolkiah Mu'izzaddin Waddaulah](/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah "Hassanal Bolkiah")
* [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei") * [Crown Prince](/wiki/Crown_Prince "Crown Prince") of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei")
[House of Salalila](/wiki/House_of_Salalila "House of Salalila") (1500–1571) [House of Digadong](/wiki/House_of_Digadong "House of Digadong") (1826–1828)

is the ruling [royal family](/wiki/Royal_family "Royal family") of [Brunei Darussalam](/wiki/Brunei_Darussalam "Brunei Darussalam"). It is composed of the descendants of the 1st sultan [Sultan Muhammad Shah](/wiki/Muhammad_Shah_of_Brunei "Muhammad Shah of Brunei") and his family. The [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei") is the [head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state") and [absolute monarch](/wiki/Absolute_monarch "Absolute monarch") of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). He is also [head of government](/wiki/Head_of_government "Head of government") in his capacity as Prime Minister.[[1]](#cite_note-official-1)

| * [v](/wiki/Template:Politics_of_Brunei "Template:Politics of Brunei") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Politics_of_Brunei "Template talk:Politics of Brunei") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Politics_of_Brunei "Special:EditPage/Template:Politics of Brunei") |

The **House of Bolkiah** is the ruling [royal family](/wiki/Royal_family "Royal family") of [Brunei Darussalam](/wiki/Brunei_Darussalam "Brunei Darussalam"). It is composed of the descendants of the 1st sultan [Sultan Muhammad Shah](/wiki/Muhammad_Shah_of_Brunei "Muhammad Shah of Brunei") and his family. The [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei") is the [head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state") and [absolute monarch](/wiki/Absolute_monarch "Absolute monarch") of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). He is also [head of government](/wiki/Head_of_government "Head of government") in his capacity as Prime Minister.[[1]](#cite_note-official-1)

Since independence from the [British](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") in 1984, only one Sultan has reigned, though the royal institution dates back to the 14th century.[[2]](#cite_note-list-2) The Sultan of Brunei can be thought of as synonymous with the ruling House of Bolkiah, with descendency being traced from the 1st Sultan [Muhammad Shah](/wiki/Muhammad_Shah_of_Brunei "Muhammad Shah of Brunei")'s brother the 2nd Sultan [Ahmad](/wiki/Sultan_Ahmad_of_Brunei "Sultan Ahmad of Brunei") through and his daughter Puteri Ratna Kesuma, the wife of the 3rd Sultan [Sharif Ali](/wiki/Sharif_Ali "Sharif Ali"), a [Sayyid](/wiki/Sayyid "Sayyid") and grandson of Emir [Rumaythah ibn Abi Numayy](/wiki/Rumaythah_ibn_Abi_Numayy "Rumaythah ibn Abi Numayy") of Mecca. The 13th Sultan [Abdul Hakkul Mubin](/wiki/Abdul_Hakkul_Mubin "Abdul Hakkul Mubin") was an exception to familial succession, but he ascended after murdering the 12th Sultan [Muhammad Ali](/wiki/Muhammad_Ali_(Brunei) "Muhammad Ali (Brunei)") and was in turn killed by the 14th Sultan [Muhyiddin](/wiki/Muhyiddin_of_Brunei "Muhyiddin of Brunei"). It is not clear when the house became known as the 'House of Bolkiah', and whether it was named after the current 29th Sultan [Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah "Hassanal Bolkiah"), or the 5th Sultan [Bolkiah](/wiki/Bolkiah "Bolkiah").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Title

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=House_of_Bolkiah&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Title")]

See also: [Style (form of address) § Brunei](/wiki/Style_(form_of_address)#Brunei "Style (form of address)")

The full title of the Sultan is: ***His Majesty* The Sultan and [Yang Di-Pertuan](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertuan_Negara "Yang di-Pertuan Negara") of Brunei Darussalam** (Kebawah Duli Yang Maha Mulia Paduka Seri Baginda Sultan dan Yang di-Pertuan Negara Brunei Darussalam).[[1]](#cite_note-official-1)

Today, the surname Bolkiah is carried by any descendant of the House of Bolkiah. Members of the royal household hold high and influential positions in government.

Sons and grandsons of Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah are referred to in the style "His Royal Highness" (HRH) or "*Pengiran Muda*" in Malay, differing from the royals belonging to the cadet branches which are addressed as "The Lord" (HH) or "*Pengiran Anak*" in Malay.

## Members

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=House_of_Bolkiah&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Members")]

The core of the Brunei royal family is made up of the immediate family of the incumbent Sultan of Brunei;

* [Sultan Hasannal Bolkiah of Brunei](/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah "Hassanal Bolkiah") and [Queen Saleha of Brunei](/wiki/Queen_Saleha_of_Brunei "Queen Saleha of Brunei")
  + [Princess Rashidah Sa'adatul Bolkiah](/wiki/Princess_Rashidah_Sa%27adatul_Bolkiah "Princess Rashidah Sa'adatul Bolkiah") and [Abdul Rahim Kemaluddin](/wiki/Pengiran_Anak_Abdul_Rahim "Pengiran Anak Abdul Rahim")
  + [Princess Muta-Wakkilah Hayatul Bolkiah](/wiki/Princess_Muta-Wakkilah_Hayatul_Bolkiah "Princess Muta-Wakkilah Hayatul Bolkiah")
  + [Crown Prince Al-Muhtadee Billah](/wiki/Al-Muhtadee_Billah "Al-Muhtadee Billah") and [Crown Princess Sarah](/wiki/Sarah,_Crown_Princess_of_Brunei "Sarah, Crown Princess of Brunei")
    - [Prince Abdul Muntaqim](/wiki/Prince_Abdul_Muntaqim_of_Brunei "Prince Abdul Muntaqim of Brunei")
    - [Princess Muneerah Madhul Bolkiah](/wiki/Princess_Muneerah_Madhul_Bolkiah "Princess Muneerah Madhul Bolkiah")
    - [Prince Muhammad Aiman](/wiki/Prince_Muhammad_Aiman_of_Brunei "Prince Muhammad Aiman of Brunei")
    - Princess Faathimah Az-Zahraa' Raaihanul
  + [Princess Majeedah Nuurul Bolkiah](/wiki/Princess_Majeedah_Nuurul_Bolkiah "Princess Majeedah Nuurul Bolkiah")
  + [Princess Hafizah Sururul Bolkiah](/wiki/Princess_Hafizah_Sururul_Bolkiah "Princess Hafizah Sururul Bolkiah") and [Muhammad Ruzaini Mohd. Yakub](/wiki/Pengiran_Anak_Muhammad_Ruzaini "Pengiran Anak Muhammad Ruzaini")
  + [Prince Abdul Malik](/wiki/Prince_Abdul_Malik_of_Brunei "Prince Abdul Malik of Brunei") and [Raabi'atul 'Adawiyyah Bolkiah](/wiki/Raabi%27atul_Adawiyyah "Raabi'atul Adawiyyah")
  + [Princess Azemah Ni'matul Bolkiah](/wiki/Princess_Azemah_Ni%27matul_Bolkiah "Princess Azemah Ni'matul Bolkiah") and [Prince Bahar](/wiki/Pengiran_Muda_Bahar "Pengiran Muda Bahar")
  + [Princess Fadzilah Lubabul Bolkiah](/wiki/Princess_Fadzilah_Lubabul_Bolkiah "Princess Fadzilah Lubabul Bolkiah") and [Abdullah Al-Hashimi](/wiki/Abdullah_Al-Hashimi "Abdullah Al-Hashimi")
  + [Prince Abdul Mateen](/wiki/Prince_Abdul_Mateen_of_Brunei "Prince Abdul Mateen of Brunei") and [Anisha Isa Kalebic](/wiki/Anisha_Rosnah "Anisha Rosnah")
  + [Prince Abdul Wakeel](/wiki/Prince_Abdul_Wakeel_of_Brunei "Prince Abdul Wakeel of Brunei")
  + [Princess Ameerah Wardatul Bolkiah](/wiki/Princess_Ameerah_Wardatul_Bolkiah "Princess Ameerah Wardatul Bolkiah")

Only Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah and his two sons; Crown Prince Al-Muhtadee Billah and Prince Abdul Mateen carry out government duties full-time, mainly for the [Prime Minister's Office](/wiki/Prime_Minister%27s_Office_(Brunei) "Prime Minister's Office (Brunei)") and the [Royal Brunei Armed Forces](/wiki/Royal_Brunei_Armed_Forces "Royal Brunei Armed Forces"). The Sultan's sister, Princess Masna, is the current [ambassador-at-large](/wiki/Ambassador-at-large "Ambassador-at-large") to the [United Nations](/wiki/United_Nations "United Nations") representing the Brunei government and the only extended royal member to be part of the working royals.

Other members of the royal family with royal rank who do not carry out official duties are the Sultan's siblings, and their children are considered to be part of the lesser royal members;

* [Prince Mohamed Bolkiah](/wiki/Prince_Mohamed_Bolkiah "Prince Mohamed Bolkiah")
* [Princess Masna](/wiki/Princess_Masna_of_Brunei "Princess Masna of Brunei")
* [Princess Nor'ain](/wiki/Princess_Nor%27ain_of_Brunei "Princess Nor'ain of Brunei")
* [Prince Sufri Bolkiah](/wiki/Prince_Sufri_Bolkiah "Prince Sufri Bolkiah")
* [Prince Jefri Bolkiah](/wiki/Prince_Jefri_Bolkiah "Prince Jefri Bolkiah")
* Princess Umi Kalthum Al-Islam
* Princess Amal Rakiah
* Princess Amal Nasibah
* Princess Amal Jefriah
